channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: Squier Affinity Precision Bass PJ 2020 - Race Red — $299
item.description:
$299 · Good · GUITAR WARS

Used but plays and sounds great. Minor wear and tear all over but nothing serious or damaging. No case or bag. Any questions feel free to ask

item.title: Fender American Vintage Reissue '62 AVRI Precision P Bass Black NItrocellulose Nitro Finish — $1,700
item.description:
$1,700 · Good · Tower Guitar & Bass Shop

EVERYTHING MUST GO! I am retiring from music and all of my instruments are being sold (except for a cheap nylon string to satisfy the urge should it arise). This is everything that I own, please feel free to ask any questions and I will do my best to answer them.

This listing is for an AVRI '62 P bass in good, used shape. It plays great and sounds like a classic P bass, it comes with a case and it appears that the pickguard was likely replaced as I think these guys originally came with tortoise shell.


  • Body Material: Alder
  • Body Finish: Nitrocellulose lacquer
  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Neck Profile: 1960 "C" or "Large C" shape
  • Scale Length: 34”
  • Fingerboard Material: Rosewood
  • Fingerboard Radius: 7.25"
  • Number of Frets: 20 vintage-style frets
  • Nut Width: \(1\frac{3}{4}\)" (1.735” to 1.75” depending on the specific production year)
  • Nut Material: Bone
  • Pickups: American Vintage '62 (or '58/'63 depending on the exact run) split single-coil Precision Bass
  • Controls: Master Volume, Master Tone
  • Bridge: 4-Saddle American Vintage with threaded steel "barrel" saddles
  • Tuning Machines: Vintage-style reverse open-gear

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man Sterling 4 H with Rosewood Fretboard 2016 - 2018 - Chili Red — $1,400
item.description:
$1,400 · Excellent · Jon's Gear Garage

Up for grabs is an excellent USA Sterling 4H. Plays and sounds nicel!

item.title: Human Base BaseX Unknown - Quilted Glossy Red — $922.48
item.description:
$922.48 · Very Good · Dave's Dandy Deals

This exceptionally rare beauty has an incredible look, build quality and most importantly sound. Information is limited on these German made and designed instruments other than that they were available with a variety of options and finishes and were highly regarded for their stunning appearance and performance. Equipped with active/passive Delano Humbuckers.

This Human Base bass guitar has incredible action and has recently been given a complete servicing and everything works as it should.

This guitar turns a lot of heads, don't let it slip by! Comes with a padded soft case which will be shipped inside a padded box.

item.title: Ken Smith BSR5 2022 Bass Guitar — $7,299
item.description:
$7,299 · Very Good · BAMonBASS Gear Emporium

2022 Ken Smith BSR5 with walnut body wings and a maple core, it has the 18 volt Smith preamp and hardshell case.

item.title: Sterling StingRay Ray35HH 2019 - 2023 - Daphne Blue — $670
item.description:
$670 · Excellent · Atmosphere Audio Store

Up for sale is my Sterling by Music Man StingRay Ray5 in the beautiful Daphne Blue finish. It’s in excellent condition and has been well cared for in a smoke-free home.

Everything works exactly as it should. The neck is straight, the truss rod functions properly, the frets are in excellent shape, and the action is low and comfortable. The electronics are clean and quiet, making this bass ready for gigs, rehearsals, or studio sessions.

The StingRay humbucker delivers the punchy, articulate tone these basses are known for, with plenty of low-end and versatility thanks to the onboard active preamp.

Features

  • Sterling by Music Man StingRay Ray5
  • Daphne Blue finish
  • 5-string
  • Active electronics
  • Maple neck and maple fretboard
  • Sterling Music Man humbucker
  • Chrome hardware
  • Comfortable, fast-playing neck

Condition

  • Excellent cosmetic condition
  • Minimal signs of use
  • No cracks, repairs, or structural issues
  • Freshly cleaned and ready to play

Includes

  • Original Sterling by Music Man gig bag (pictured)

I’m only selling because I’m downsizing my collection and making room for other gear. Feel free to message me with any questions or if you’d like additional photos.

Thanks for looking!

item.title: Brubaker Steve Jenkins 5 2022 - Black — $3,300
item.description:
$3,300 · Excellent · R2 Musical

9 lbs
2022 Build
Alder body
Maple neck/fingerboard
Black block inlays
Aguilar DCB Pups
John East JRetro preamp 
(BOB interchangeable preamp system)
Near Mint condition 
Hipshot Black Hardware 
Kickass Bridge
Ultra lite Tuners
Dunlop straploks

item.title: ESP LTD Surveyor '87 Electric Bass Rainbow Crackle — $1,099
item.description:
$1,099 · Brand New · LV Guitars

ESP LTD Surveyor '87 Electric Bass Rainbow Crackle

The Surveyor '87 delivers a balanced, ergonomic platform with its thin U neck contour and moderate 9 lbs, 12 oz weight — comfortable for extended playing sessions without fatigue. The Rainbow Crackle finish commands visual presence while the bolt-on construction and classic proportions ground this instrument in proven bass fundamentals. Built around vintage-voiced electronics, this bass voices warm, articulate low end suitable for vintage-influenced and contemporary styles alike.

Playability & Build

The 34" scale neck pairs a thin U contour with Macassar ebony fingerboard and 21 XJ frets for smooth, fast navigation across the neck. Neck dimensions measure .760" at the 1st fret and .840" at the 12th fret, establishing a comfortable grip, while the 42mm molded nut and 400mm fingerboard radius support both fingerstyle and pick-driven approaches.

Tone & Electronics

The Seymour Duncan Vintage P and Vintage Jazz pickup pairing delivers warm, full-bodied tone with articulate high-end clarity — classic voicing that cuts through without harshness. Passive electronics with Volume/Balance/Tone controls and Gotoh 201-B4 bridge provide reliable hardware and straightforward tone-shaping, while D'Addario XL165 strings (.045/.065/.085/.105) arrive pre-installed.

Key Features
  • Bolt-on alder body with maple neck and Macassar ebony fingerboard
  • 34" scale length with 42mm molded nut
  • Thin U neck contour; .760" at 1st fret, .840" at 12th fret
  • 21 XJ frets with 400mm fingerboard radius
  • Rainbow Crackle finish with black hardware
  • Seymour Duncan Vintage P (neck) and Vintage Jazz (bridge) pickups
  • Passive electronics: Volume/Balance/Tone layout
  • Gotoh 201-B4 bridge and LTD Vintage tuners
  • D'Addario XL165 strings pre-installed (.045/.065/.085/.105)
  • 9 lbs, 12 oz total weight

The Surveyor '87 combines ergonomic design, vintage-style electronics, and straightforward hardware into a versatile platform suited to players seeking authentic tone and reliable playability.

item.title: Schecter Stiletto Custom 4 Lusithand NFP loaded — $650
item.description:
$650 · Very Good · Brian's Used Equipment

Schecter Stiletto Custom 4 made in Korea modified with a Lusithand double NFP MkII preamp.

This preamp is very similar to a Wal/Alembic type preamp with a parametric filter for each pickup, pickup blend, and master volume. Tonally, there is an inherent aggressive character as it’s a bit higher gain than the original EMG preamp, but a lot more tonal variety between pickups can be achieved with the filters and blend working together.

This bass also originally had painted gold hardware, but most has been changed to black besides the strap buttons. The individual bridges are the same type as the OEM bridges but black. Original tuners were cheap Gotoh machine heads but have since been changed with Hipshot Ultralites with mini clover keys. Knobs are aluminum sourced from Lusithand.

item.title: Warwick Thumb BO 6 2023 — $2,500
item.description:
$2,500 · Excellent · Matthew's Gear Outlet

I’m sad to see this one go but wrist issues and a need for money has left me no choice. The bass is a 2023 Thumb Bolt on German Pro Series Six String. It’s in excellent condition and has recently been waxed and PLEK’d. I purchased it new in January 2026. The bass has no wear and plays like a dream. It comes with a Warwick Gig Bag and a can of Warwick surface finisher. $2,500 – buyer pays shipping costs.

item.title: Kay K-5915 Speed Demon Bass - Sunburst — $1,600
item.description:
$1,600 · Very Good · Benny’s Guitars

Beautiful ‘66 vintage Kay K-5915 Speed Demon semi-hollow body bass Guitar. I am the third owner and the gentleman I purchased it from bought the bass back in 1967 and has taken very good care of it. It is fully functional, the electronics work and it sounds gorgeous. I had the neck straightened at Guitar Center, and the intonation is very good on it. Very slight buzzing on the high end of the G string, but can be adjusted to player taste using bridge adjuster. Has the famous speed bump pickup that produces that unique, vibey, thumpy sound that vintage Kay basses from this era are known for. The checkerboard binding is in pristine condition. Comes with original chipboard case. If there was ever a beautiful specimen of a K-5915, this is it! This is your chance to own this piece of Kay history. The bass does have some blemishes and discoloration in the finish that is expected for a 59-year-old bass.

item.title: Kala PSGR-FS U-Bass Passenger Fretted Mahogany Ukulele Bass 2010s - Satin Natural — $150
item.description:
$150 · Very Good · Keep Portland Geared

Kala’s popular U-Bass ukulele-sized bass is made of mahogany with a satin finish and comes equipped with black die-cast tuners, Aquila Thundergut strings and a Kala UK-500B preamp with EQ and built-in tuner.

The Passenger model adds a white binding and rosette for stylish ornamentation. The Journeyman sets itself apart with a cutaway body shape and f-holes, and retailed at $299.99.

This one is in great condition with no major nicks or dings - see the photos for condition details. I noticed that the G-string output was a little lower than the other strings, but it pulled off its gigs well.

I mainly used this to record indoor live streams during COVID and so it was played indoors. The original strings were worn out and replaced with Kala replacements. These new strings do not have much play on them and are good to go.

Also comes with original gig bag as shipped from Kala (double backpack straps make it easy to transport).

Kala Passenger Acoustic-Electric U-Bass Specs:
Top: Mahogany
Back & Sides: Mahogany
Binding: Cream
Finish: Satin
Neck: Mahogany
Nut: Plastic
Saddle: Uk-500B Piezo Pickup
Headstock: Standard
Strings: Aquila Thundergut®
Tuners: Custom Black Die Cast
Electronics: Uk-500B
Short Scale: 20.875 in
Overall Length: 29.625 in
Includes: Padded Gig Bag

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass 3-Bolt with Rosewood Fretboard 1974 - 1983 Olympic White — $2,300
item.description:
$2,300 · Excellent · David 's Boutique

Here is a beautiful looking American Fender Jazz Bass in excellent condition .Pick up replaced with lundgren pick up (kept original pick up). Having them rewound . The Lundgren pups sound great. the tone pot was replaced . Comes with tweed Fender case in great condition.Comes with original bridge chrome cover. Bass weighs around 10 lbs . Body and neck are great condition. The neck has the serial number from 1978 which was common on these 83 jazz basses . They used leftover parts ,, but is stamped with date of 1983 on heel. I have the original white pup covers also to go with bass. Right now the black covers came with Lundgren pickups when purchased

item.title: Vintage 1996 Ibanez SR 505 Black 5 String Bass Guitar — $699
item.description:
$699 · Very Good · Amiricol Music

Vintage 1996. Yes, this is 30 years old! Black Ibanez SR-505 5 String bass. These are amazing basses that are still very reasonable. Active electronics with bass, treble and a sweepable mid control. Bass is in excellent condition. Comes w/ the original black plastic Ibanez hard case. One latch is broken and some feet are pushed in on the bottom but it still works 🙂 Made in Korea.

item.title: Silvertone 1444 1959-1960 - Black — $1,495
item.description:
$1,495 · Good · Jeanette 's Boutique

Here’s an opportunity to own one of the most iconic vintage short-scale basses ever produced. This original Silvertone Model 1444 was built by Danelectro for Sears during the late 1950s and features the highly collectible “Dolphin” headstock, Masonite construction, and legendary lipstick pickup that helped define countless recordings.

This bass has just received a professional setup with a fresh set of strings and is ready to play. It has the unmistakable warm, punchy vintage Danelectro tone with outstanding clarity and exceptional low-end response.

Features

  • Original Danelectro-built Silvertone Model 1444
  • Circa 1959–1960
  • Rare Dolphin headstock
  • Original lipstick pickup
  • Original Vinyl sides
  • Original potentiometers and wiring
  • Original bridge with rosewood saddle
  • Original Kluson tuners
  • Masonite body over poplar frame
  • Short-scale neck
  • Professionally set up with new strings

Condition

Overall, the bass is in very good vintage condition and plays beautifully.

The original pickguard is missing. There are two small filled holes on the body from a previously installed Fender thumb rest that has since been removed. These are cosmetic only and do not affect playability or tone.

The finish shows honest vintage wear consistent with age, adding to its character without detracting from its performance.

This bass delivers the unmistakable vintage Danelectro sound that has made these instruments favorites among studio musicians, collectors, and players for more than six decades.

A wonderful original example that’s ready for its next collection or stage.

Keywords: Vintage Silvertone Bass, Danelectro Bass, Model 1444, Dolphin Headstock, Lipstick Pickup, Masonite Bass, Sears Bass, Short Scale Bass, 1959 Silvertone, 1960 Silvertone, Vintage American Bass, Collectible Bass Guitar.

item.title: ESP LTD JC-4 John Campbell Dark Grey Metallic Satin — $2,099
item.description:
$2,099 · Brand New · Studio Gears

ESP LTD JC-4 John Campbell Dark Grey Metallic Satin

As a founding member of Lamb of God, John Campbell has been delivering the powerful low end for this famed American heavy metal band since 1994. His LTD Signature Series JC-4 bass offers all the quality, playability, and tone essential for any music where the bass is a cornerstone. Based on the ESP Stream body shape, the JC-4 combines professional-grade components with a sleek design to meet the demands of serious musicians.

Key Features:
  • Swamp Ash Body: The JC-4 features a swamp ash body, providing a balanced, resonant tone with a strong low end and crisp highs.
  • 5-Piece Maple/Purple Heart Neck: The bolt-on neck construction, made of a five-piece maple/purple heart blend, offers stability and a fast-playing thin-U contour for excellent playability.
  • Macassar Ebony Fingerboard: The Macassar ebony fingerboard includes 21 extra-jumbo frets, split block inlays, and glow-in-the-dark side markers for a visually striking and functional design.
  • Fishman Fluence SB-1 Pickups: Equipped with versatile Fishman Fluence SB-1 active pickups, the JC-4 delivers a dynamic range and articulation. The pickups feature multiple voicings, accessible via a 3-way mini toggle switch and a push-pull volume control for single-coil mode.
  • High-End Components: This bass includes Hipshot Ultralight tuners and a Hipshot A Style bridge with slotted saddles, ensuring reliable performance and precise intonation.
Specifications:
  • Construction: Bolt-On
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Body: Swamp Ash
  • Neck: 5-piece Maple/Purple Heart
  • Fingerboard: Macassar Ebony
  • Fingerboard Radius: 400mm
  • Finish: Dark Grey Metallic Satin
  • Nut Width: 38mm (Molded)
  • Frets: 21 XJ (Extra Jumbo)
  • Hardware Color: Black
  • Strap Button: Standard
  • Tuners: Hipshot Ultralite
  • Bridge: Hipshot A Style with Slotted Saddles
  • Pickups: Fishman Fluence SB-1 (Neck and Bridge, Pull Volume for Single Coil Mode)
  • Electronics Layout: Volume (Push/Pull)/Balance/Fishman 2-Band EQ (Stacked)/3-Way Mini Switch Voice Select
  • Strings: D'Addario XL165 (.045/.065/.085/.105)
  • Case: Stream Bass Form Fit Case (Included)
Perfect for Professional Bassists

The ESP LTD JC-4 John Campbell is ideal for bassists who need a high-performance instrument capable of delivering versatile tones and reliable performance. Its combination of innovative technology, high-quality components, and sleek design makes it perfect for professional musicians who demand the best.

Whether you’re performing live, recording in the studio, or practicing at home, the JC-4 provides the performance and versatility you need. Experience the exceptional quality and tone of the ESP LTD JC-4 John Campbell Bass in Dark Grey Metallic Satin!

item.title: Squier Vintage Modified Bass VI 2014 - 2018 - Black — $645
item.description:
$645 · Excellent · Adverse Effectors

This Squier Bass VI is like new. It sounds and plays great! It will benefit from fresh strings and a setup. I'll take more photos as soon as I can.

item.title: Fender Vintera II 70s Telecaster Bass - Vintage White — $1,033.17
item.description:
$1,033.17 · Good · Wentworth Music

New Inventory, listed as used, as bass was dropped by customer. Full manufacturers warranty.

FEATURES

  • Alder Body
  • 7.25″ Radius Maple Fingerboard with Vintage Tall Frets
  • Mid-’70s “C”-Shape Neck
  • Vintage-Style Wide-Range Bass Humbucker
  • Vintage-Style 2-Saddle Bridge
  • Vintage-Style “Fender” Stamped Tuning Machines

The Vintera® II ’70s Telecaster® Bass features an alder body and a maple neck for classic Fender tone that’s full of punch and clarity. The mid-’70s “C”-shape neck is based on a classic ’70s profile for an intuitive and inviting feel, while the 7.25″ radius fingerboard with vintage-tall frets provide vintage comfort with ample room for big bends and expressive vibrato. Under the hood, the vintage-style wide-range bass humbucker delivers the iconic Fender sound: warm, woody, dynamic and powerful. The vintage-style 2-saddle bridge and vintage-style “fender”-stamped tuning machines provide classic looks with enhanced intonation and tuning stability to complete the package.

Experience the unmistakable vintage feel and unmatched sound of a classic Fender with the Vintera® II ’70s Telecaster® Bass today and start making music history!

ALDER BODY

A classic Fender tonewood known for resonance, punch and clarity, alder has a balanced and lively tone with beefy mids and a robust low-end.

7.25” RADIUS MAPLE FINGERBOARD WITH VINTAGE TALL FRETS

The 7.25” radius fingerboard provides vintage comfort and feel, while vintage-tall frets provide slightly more height than vintage for big bends and wide vibrato.

MID-’70S “C”-SHAPE NECK

Based on a mid-’70s “C” profile, this comfortably rounded “C” has a slight taper toward the nut for feel that’s beefy but not cumbersome.

VINTAGE-STYLE WIDE-RANGE BASS HUMBUCKER

Voiced for enhanced clarity while reproducing the thick muscular punch of a vintage Tele® Bass, this Humbucker packs a mighty wallop with thick and deep lows, potent mids and a smooth, percussive top. This ‘70s spec humbucker is full of the tone that made Fender instruments famous.

VINTAGE-STYLE 2-SADDLE BRIDGE

One of the most distinctive features of the Telecaster® Bass, this rock-solid string-through-body design features two adjustable slotted steel “barrel” saddles. This vintage-correct arrangement enhances sustain and resonance.

VINTAGE-STYLE “FENDER” STAMPED TUNING MACHINES

These tuning machines offer the look and feel of vintage machines with a finer gear ratio and enhanced tuning stability.

item.title: Zon Sonus Custom Fretless 5-String (2002) - Spalted Maple Gallery Top (Gloss) — $2,450
item.description:
$2,450 · Very Good · Vince on Reverb

OVERVIEW

This is a 2002 Zon Sonus 5 Custom in truly excellent (near mint) condition, with hard case. Please note these dynamics up front:
* This was originally a lined fretless, but in 2013, I asked Joe Zon to remove the lines on the front. Therefore, the side markers are for a lined fretless, but the front is unlined. The fingerboard is as new (unlined).
* One screw in the rear battery compartment is not as snug as the others.
* Other than those two factors, this bass guitar looks brand new!!

Note: If my current impressions are accurate, the Zon Sonus "Special" typically has a bubinga topwood, because Joe Zon designed the Sonus Special to be the quintessential "Jaco" bass guitar. The Custom is very close to the Special, but with more options for topwoods. Essentially, think of this bass as being a Jaco-sounding bass guitar with gallery spalted maple topwood, and the subtle nuances of maple instead of bubinga.

PACKAGE FEATURES AND COMPONENTS (pardon the repetition in places)
Other niceties:
* Includes THREE SETS OF STRINGS. 1. Brand new flatwound Dunlop steel strings (.40 - .125) just installed, never played (part number DBFS40120). 2. D'Addario XL Chromes ECB81-5, Regular Light Gauge (package opened, but strings never installed). 3. Black tapewound strings, installed, removed, hardly played, and not broken in. These are the strings shown in most pics.
* Gallery Spalted Maple Top (gloss finish, which looks spectacular)
* ZB 4.3 active electronics with 3-way mid-frequency toggle switch.
* Includes a locking Zon hardshell case (with keys) in outstanding condition. Very tiny mark on the under side from bushing against something that was painted green, but this mark will likely come off. The case nearly looks new, except perhaps for some fading of the Zon text on the front. The interior is immaculate.
* Headstock is pristine (mint condition).

  • 34" scale, 9.2 lbs (without strap)
  • If buying an unlined fretless Zon Custom today, you would have to pay $7190.
  • I'm listing this wondrous beast for $2400. Considering the condition, the make, and the gallery top, and that it is USA made, I believe it will sell quickly.
  • Get up on stage. Everybody will think it is brand new.

Zon Website Description

Originally deemed a "Jazz Bass for the 90s," this model embodies the classic tones so popular among bassists through the world of music. In developing this bass, we sought out and employed components that would allow this model to emulate the voice of the early '60s vintage J-basses. To that end, we chose ash for its punch and clarity. Next, we employed two linear singlecoil pickups (voiced to replicate the tone of old single coils). Coupled with a two-band active system designed to emulate the sound of an old Dual Showman preamp, the Sonus has a serious classic vibe and sound.

Flawless spalted maple topwood in high gloss.

  • Brand new condition unlined fretless fingerboard
  • Head is in absolutely perfect condition
  • Brand new tapewound strings (not broken in)
  • Locking Zon hardshell case (with keys) in mint condition.

BACKGROUND & CONTEXT (some of this information is repeated from above)

 I am the second owner, and bought it in 2006. This instrument has always been protected and preserved in my care to preserve collector quality. 

Now that said, this is a fantastic player's bass. From 2006 to 2013, i would occasionally practice on it at home, just light tinkering, but always used my other Zon for rehearsals and performances. This bass truly brings a sense that it wants to play itself, and that struck me from the very first time i played on it. I am not a fretless player, so i preserved the condition of this instrument, never intending to sell it.

In 2013, I had Zon Guitars in Redwood City service the instrument with these changes:
• Completely refurbished the fretless fingerboard (it's truly like new now, and hardly played on since the refinish, and always with flatwound strings, never roundwound). 

• Transformed the bass from lined fretless to unlined fretless. It truly looks nicer this way.
    — IMPORTANT NOTE ABOUT SIDE MARKERS: The markers on the side are for lined fretless. This bass does not have conventional fretless markers on the side.
    — Why did I do this? I bought the bass because it was USA made and fretless, then the Spalted Maple top seduced me. But in fact, when playing any bass guitar, I never stoop over to look at the front of my fingerboard, so i wanted to have the lines removed. I never intended to sell this bass, but have my eye on another Zon...

• CAVEAT: One screw for the rear battery compartment fits a little loosely, but keeps its position.

• See the corresponding price for a new Zon Sonus 5 custom from the Zon form attached. For other general information, refer to the Zon website:  

Buyer pays the first $50 in shipping (included in the baseline cost). I will cover additional shipping costs and insurance to ship in continental USA (only). No layaways. No returns. Signature Required.

Note about the box: I have opted for the 52" box so I can include extra padding on all sides. I want to ensure the package gets to its destiny in perfect shape.

This is a world-class and USA-made Zon Sonus 5. Buyer should fully understand the league of this instrument.

p.s. If one thing happens in my life, this beast is coming off the market pronto, never to appear again. (Unless it sells first).

item.title: 2021 Epiphone Jack Casady Bass Faded Pelham Blue — $599
item.description:
$599 · Excellent · Mom and Dad’s Music

This 2021 Epiphone Jack Casady Bass is in excellent condition and shows only extremely light signs of play wear. This bass is just exceptionally tidy and does not appear as if it has seen a lot of play time. This bass is set up in excellent playing order and sounds fantastic!

From Epiphone: The Jack Casady Signature Bass was designed by Rock and Roll Hall of Fame legend Jack Casady and is the culmination of years of experimentation by the Jefferson Airplane and Hot Tuna bassist to find an instrument with superior electric tone and the response of an acoustic bass. Featuring the Casady-designed JCB-1 Low-Impedance Humbucker™ pickup and a three-position rotary impedance control switch for a wide range of tonal versatility.

Body Specs
Back / Layered Maple
Binding / Single-ply Cream - top and back
Body Finish / Gloss
Body Material / Layered Maple
Body Shape / Jack Casady Bass
Body Styles / Archtop
Side / Layered Maple
Top / Layered Maple

Neck Specs
Fingerboard Material / Laurel
Fingerboard Radius / 305.0mm / 12.01in
Fret Count / 20
Frets / Medium Jumbo
Inlays / Trapezoid
Joint / Glued in, Set Neck
Neck Material / Mahogany
Nut Material / Imitation Bone
Nut Width / 41.5mm / 1.63in
Profile / C
Scale Length / 863.6mm / 34.0in

Electronics Specs
Controls / 1 Volume, 1 Tone, 3-way rotary impedance selector switch
Middle Pickup / JCB-1 Low-Impedance Humbucker
Output Jack / 1/4" Heavy-Duty Epiphone

Hardware Specs
Bridge / 3 point adjustable
Control Knobs / Gold Speed knobs
Finish / Nickel
Mounting Rings / Cream
Pickguard / Casady Cream with raised E logo
Pickup Covers / Cream
Strap Buttons / 2 - bottom and back of heel
Switch Tip / Black Chicken Head knob
Switchwasher / Cream with 50, 250, 500 in Gold hot stamp
Tailpiece / 3 point adjustable
Truss Rod / Dual Action
Tuner Plating / Nickel
Tuning Machines / Die Cast - Clover buttons

Misc Specs
Headstock / Epiphone Sloped Dovewing (Bass)
Headstock Inlay / Epiphone logo in Mother of Pearl, Jack Casady signature in Gold silkscreen print

item.title: Ernie Ball MusicMan StingRay Ball Family Reserve — $2,950
item.description:
$2,950 · Excellent · BassPlace Space

An excellent bass, this StingRay HH is finished in Dargie Delight green, has an ebony fretboard with Mother of Pearl side lines. It plays great and is in mint condition! All original except the strings. Has T.I. Flats and in my opinion, they sound great! Comes with excellent condition case, COA and all the case candy!


Great deal on this: $2950. Shipping will be $50 anywhere in CONUS!

item.title: ESP LTD B-206SM Natural Satin 6 String Bass — $389
item.description:
$389 · Very Good · rEvolve Guitar and Music Shop

Up for sale is a used 2014 ESP LTD B206SM 6 String Bass. Very good plus condition with some light hardware aging and some light marks on the pickup covers. No large dings or scratches. Really nice shape for its age. Minimal fretwear. Bass is heavy at 12 lb 2 oz. Includes gig bag. Shipping is $89 anywhere in the continental US. Thank you for looking!

item.title: Josey Old Mexico 2" Guitar Strap w/Chocolate Ends and Cotton Backing — $70
item.description:
$70 · Brand New · Chicago Music Exchange

Step into the warmth of desert sunsets and roadside cantinas with the Old Mexico guitar strap from Josey Straps. Featuring a bold chevron pattern in earthy oranges, mustard yellows, rustic blues, and sage greens, this strap radiates Southwestern charm with a vintage soul. Woven for durability and accented with full-grain leather ends, Old Mexico isn’t just a strap — it’s a story of music, travel, and timeless style. Whether you’re strumming classic ballads or blazing through riffs, this strap brings color, comfort, and character to every performance.

If you happen to own an amp wrapped in Old Mexico by our friends at Benson Amps, you have to have this matching strap!  

You will be prompted to select your strap ends once you add the strap to your cart.

Available end options are Chocolate – Leather (Natural & Rustic), Saddle – Leather (Finished Auburn/Cognac), Sunset – Leather (Finished Dark Brown w/ Maroon Undertones), Midnight – Leather (Finished Black), and Onyx – Polyurethane (Matte Black Vegan-Friendly).

Strap width is 2”

Strap length is adjustable from approximately 35” to 61” (keyhole to keyhole).

All our straps come standard with durable, quality fabric stitched on a heavy duty poly backing, outfitted with nickel plated hardware and leather ends.

For musicians looking for a natural, softer material that provides more shoulder cushion and a grippier texture, you can upgrade the backing to a heavier weight cotton, perfect to help solve neck dive..

The unique cut of your strap may vary due to the nature of patterned fabric, but we will make every attempt to showcase the different possible variations.

item.title: Josey Miami Vice 2" Guitar Strap w/Saddle Ends and Cotton Backing — $70
item.description:
$70 · Brand New · Chicago Music Exchange

Turn up the heat with the Miami Vice guitar strap. Featuring bold pink flamingos, this strap channels the effortless cool of palm trees, neon lights, and ocean breezes. Handcrafted with durable webbing and finished with rich leather ends, Miami Vice isn’t just a strap — it’s a statement piece. Built for comfort, strength, and style, Miami Vice lets your guitar game strut as hard as you do.

You will be prompted to select your strap ends once you add the strap to your cart.

Available end options are Chocolate – Leather (Natural & Rustic), Saddle – Leather (Finished Auburn/Cognac), Sunset – Leather (Finished Dark Brown w/ Maroon Undertones), Midnight – Leather (Finished Black), and Onyx – Polyurethane (Matte Black Vegan-Friendly).

Strap width is 2”

Strap length is adjustable from approximately 35” to 61” (keyhole to keyhole).

All our straps come standard with durable, quality fabric stitched on a heavy duty poly backing, outfitted with nickel plated hardware and leather ends.

For musicians looking for a natural, softer material that provides more shoulder cushion and a grippier texture, you can upgrade the backing to a heavier weight cotton, perfect to help solve neck dive.

The unique cut of your strap may vary due to the nature of patterned fabric, but we will make every attempt to showcase the different possible variations.

item.title: Josey Bubblegum Tolex 2" Guitar Strap w/Chocolate Ends and Cotton Backing — $70
item.description:
$70 · Brand New · Chicago Music Exchange

Features:

  • Bright, punchy, and unapologetically pink!
  • Strap width is 2”
  • Strap length is adjustable from approximately 35” to 61” (keyhole to keyhole).
  • All our straps are made with durable, quality fabric / tolex stitched on a heavy duty backing, outfitted with nickel plated hardware and leather ends.
  • The unique cut of your strap may vary due to the nature of patterned fabric, but we will make every attempt to showcase the different possible variations.

item.title: Sandberg Greenline VS4 Short Scale Tobacco Sunburst High Gloss (Serial #49350) — $2,759
item.description:
$2,759 · Brand New · Chicago Music Exchange

Sandberg Guitars are handcrafted in Germany with a relentless focus on precision, playability, and tone that professional bassists and guitarists can rely on night after night. Each instrument features premium tonewoods, meticulously cut and finished, paired with high-output electronics designed to deliver punchy lows, articulate mids, and singing highs. Ergonomic neck profiles and balanced body contours make Sandberg instruments comfortable for long sets and demanding studio sessions alike. Built with obsessive German engineering standards, a Sandberg is an instrument made to perform at the highest level for a lifetime.



General:
Brand Name: Sandberg
Case Type: Gig Bag
Hardware Color: Chrome
Made in: Made in Germany
Number of Strings: 4-String
Product Color: Tobacco Sunburst
Serial Number: 49350
Weight: 7lbs 3oz

Body:
Body Finish: High Gloss
Body Material: Alder

Neck:
Fingerboard Inlay: Black Dots
Fingerboard Material: Maple
Headstock Inlay or Decal: Sandberg Logo
Neck Finish: High Gloss
Neck Joint: 6-Bolt
Neck Material: Maple
Neck Shape: C-Shape
Number of Frets: 22 Frets
Nut Material: Synthetic
Nut Width: 39.5mm
Side Marker Inlay: Dots
Truss Rod: Adjustable

Electronics:
Bridge Pickup: Sandberg Splitcoil
Controls and Switching: Active 2-Band EQ, Active/Passive, Passive Tone Control

Hardware:
Bridge: Sandberg Bridge 4
Pickguard: Tortoise
Saddle Material: Metal

item.title: Sandberg Greenline VS4 Short Scale Black High Gloss (Serial #49345) — $2,759
item.description:
$2,759 · Brand New · Chicago Music Exchange

Sandberg Guitars are handcrafted in Germany with a relentless focus on precision, playability, and tone that professional bassists and guitarists can rely on night after night. Each instrument features premium tonewoods, meticulously cut and finished, paired with high-output electronics designed to deliver punchy lows, articulate mids, and singing highs. Ergonomic neck profiles and balanced body contours make Sandberg instruments comfortable for long sets and demanding studio sessions alike. Built with obsessive German engineering standards, a Sandberg is an instrument made to perform at the highest level for a lifetime.

item.title: Kiesel A2 Custom Bass Sunburst 2025 — $2,100
item.description:
$2,100 · Very Good · Chicago Music Exchange

The following specs were carefully collected and recorded by a skilled technician. For a more detailed description and questions regarding sound, feel, or cosmetic condition, please call to speak to one of our experts.

Summary

Body Material: Alder

Top Material: Maple

Neck Material: Maple/Walnut

Neck Profile: C

Neck Thickness (IN): 1st fret - 0.830" / 12th fret - 0.905"

Fingerboard Radius: 14.00"

Nut Width: 1-5/8"

Electronics: Original pickups and wiring.

Pickup Measurements: Middle - 9.2k ohms, Bridge - 9.3k ohms

Weight: 8 lbs 11 oz

Cosmetic Condition: The bass is in very good cosmetic condition with only light scratches and scuffs in the finish. There is a small dent on the bass side lower bout of the top.

Serial Number: I63133

Tech Notes: The bass plays well with low action. The frets are tall with minimal play wear.

Case Details: Original Gig Bag

item.title: Fender Modern Player Starcaster Bass Natural 2013 — $840
item.description:
$840 · Very Good · Chicago Music Exchange

The following specs were carefully collected and recorded by a skilled technician. For a more detailed description and questions regarding sound, feel, or cosmetic condition, please call to speak to one of our experts.

Summary

Pickup Measurements: Neck - 21.1k ohms, Bridge - 21.1k ohms

Weight: 10 lbs 3 oz

Serial Number: CGF1323771

Tech Notes: The bass plays well with low action. The frets are tall with minimal play wear.

Case Details: Gig Bag

item.title: Spector B.O.B. 4 4-String Vintage Bass - SSD BOB-4 #010 — $2,290
item.description:
$2,290 · Good · Fret Nation

Pre-Owned Spector B.O.B. 4-String Bass
Model Number: BOB-4
Serial Number: #010Signed: June 18, 1996
Weight: 8.98 Pounds

This is a piece of Spector History!  The B.O.B. (Bolt On Bass) was made in very limited quantities during their production time, estimated under 150 in existance.  According to previous listings where this instrument was sold, this specific bass was still in the Woodstock facility before they moved to its current space.  


This Spector BOB has its original EMG SSD Soapbar Humbucking Pickups and has a current Spector Darkglass Tone Capsule with a 3rd micro potentiometer in the control cavity that adjusts the low mids.  

We have fully inspected this instrument and confirmed everything is functional as it should be.  The body is overall in great condition with exception of the back where it looks like it slid on the floor or something.  The neck feels fantastic but does have some very small impact dimples on it that aren't really felt while playing.  There are also a couple of screw holes from where a different type of jack plate was installed at one point.  

The bass does not include a case.  We will ship securely using a generic bag or case.  Contact us about upgrading your case! 

Any questions at all please do not hesitate to ask! 

This is a pre-owned instrument and is exempt from our new instrument return policy.  FretNation guarantees the instrument to be delivered in fully working condition and as described.

item.title: DRIVE Wildfire Bass 2000's - Short Scale 29.75" ! Cool little bass in great shape — $129.99
item.description:
$129.99 · Very Good · Lenny's Bass Guitars & Stuff

I bought this short scale bass on a whim, for a big modding project. Originally, I thought it was a bit worse-for-wear, but it is actually in very nice shape, so for now I will leave it totally stock and see if anyone has interest. It is a DRIVE Wildfire Bass, from the 2000's. Made in China and is really a solid little bass. From how I measure, it has a 29-3/4" scale and is pretty lightweight: 6lb 9.4oz. Nicely balanced and a blast to play. All electronics work fine. I will be installing a fresh set of D'addario strings before shipping it out. I should have a set on Saturday, 7/11 or thereabouts.

So, if anyone is interested, great! If not, then it will be modded extensively, Muahahahahahahaaa. OTOH, I have a ton of projects before me, so who knows how long it will sit here.

I will ship this out as soon as payment clears, via the carrier of my choice, insured and signature required.

Many Thanks for looking. Please look at all the pics, as they are also a part of the description. Feel free to contact me with questions/comments; they are most welcome.

-Lenny

item.title: Sadowsky Sadowsky Limited Edition J/J Metroline Bass, '20 — $2,495
item.description:
$2,495 · Very Good · Dave's Guitar Shop

Very Good condition, Whale Blue Transparent, Made in Germany, Satin finish, Ash body, Torrefied Maple bolt-on neck with a Torrefied Maple fingerboard, 34" scale length, 1.5" nut width, Sadowsky passive J single-coil bridge and neck pickups, 9 volt active preamp with the volume/pickup blend/passive tone/bass and treble controls, The tone pot does have a push/pull for active or passive operation, Sadowsky quick release bridge and open gear tuners, The neck thickness is .83″ at the first and .95″ at the 12th frets, 7.9 pounds, With the Sadowsky gig bag.

Condition Notes: There are small dings/dents/scratches on the body, the jack plate has been replaced with an electrosocket jack, there are some smaller dings around the edges of the headstock,

item.title: Fender Used American Professional Fretless Jazz Bass '18 — $995
item.description:
$995 · Very Good · Dave's Guitar Shop

Very Good Condition

3 Color Sunburst, Gloss Urethane body finish, Alder body, Slim C shaped Maple neck with a 9.5" radius lined fretless Rosewood fingerboard, 34" scale length, 1.5" nut width, Fender V-Mod single coil Jazz Bass bridge and neck pickups with the volume/volume/tone controls, HiMass Vintage string thru body or top load bridge, Fender lightweight vintage style tuners, 8.9 pounds, With hardshell case.

Please Note: Pickguard has been changed to black.

item.title: Warwick RockBass Corvette 6-String Fretless — $950
item.description:
$950 · Excellent · Lorenzo's Gear Depot

Mint condition Warwick RockBass Corvette 6-string in gloss black. Barely played and well cared for. Features MEC dual-coil pickups, 2-band active EQ (Volume, Balance, Treble, Bass), wenge fretboard, and chrome hardware.

  • Excellent condition with no major dings or scratches
  • Smooth, fast-playing neck
  • Great for fingerstyle, slap, or metal
  • Sounds and plays great

Price: $950 OBO

Selling because it doesn’t get played anymore. Feel free to message me with any questions or for additional photos.

item.title: Warwick German standard Series Corvette Ash 4 2005 - Natural Transparent — $1,191.31
item.description:
$1,191.31 · Good · Stu's shop

Corvette 4 string made in germany circa 2005.

Ash body

Ovangkol neck

Bell brass frets

Mec active pickups and preamp

Comes with padded gigbag and owners case

item.title: Fano Alt De Facto JM4, 2020 — $2,300
item.description:
$2,300 · Good · Peter's Gear Outlet

For sale is my FANO Alt De Facto JM4 2020

This is the top of the line model according to the Fano website: "Alt De Facto guitars are designed so you can have your own custom instrument, made to your own specifications with your choice of pickups, color, wood, hardware and finish style. Hand-built by one of our builders, these are truly individual guitars, for every individual player."

According to Graham Dowling at Fano: "You may be interested to know that your JM4 was the first JM4 made in the Alt de Facto line. I built it for display at the 2020 NAMM show as a matched pair with an Alt de Facto TC6. I have attached a copy of the spec sheet for you in the event you didn’t receive one with the bass."

This bass plays and sounds great. It has Lollar Thunderbird pickups, a bound neck, and weighs 9 lbs, 7oz. It's a great rock bass. I've attached a lot of photos so you can check out the checking and relic-ing. Note: I believe some of the relic-ing happened after it left the factory.

I will ship it in the original hardshell case, which is a very nice white and blue tweed. It is strung with La Bella Ian Martin Allison Super Polished Bass Guitar Strings - .045-.103. The action is low - ~1.5mm across all strings with ~10 thou of relief - and the truss rod functions as it should. It plays very easily.

Please let me know if you have questions.

item.title: Mike Lull P4 2016 - Seafoam Green 7.4 lbs. — $3,400
item.description:
$3,400 · Excellent · Dennis's Gear Locker

For your consideration is a beautiful Mike Lull Custom P-4 bass. I have owned this bass since 2017 and purchased it from The Bass Shop in Seattle. I was told that I was the second owner. This bass was crafted in 2016 up in Bellevue, WA, just outside of Seattle. If you like and appreciate the classic colors, you will definitely appreciate this baby. Seafoam green with a pearloid pick guard, and inlays on the frets. The fretboard is birds eye maple. It has Hipshot Ultralite tuning machines and the E tuner is a 'Drop D extender'. The pickup is a Lindy Fralin. All Mike Lull basses are PLEKed prior to leaving the factory. This bass plays like a dream and has that vintage p-bass thump. The instruments that come from Mike Lull are extremely well made and a joy to play. The bass weighs 7.4 lbs, measured on a bathroom scale, and comes with a Protec case with the Mike Lull logo.

I only sell and ship to the lower 48 Sates. Please look at the photos and feel free to reach out with any questions.

From the Mike Lull website:

"Mike originally designed our P4 with the idea of replacing his personal 1964 P-Bass to play at gigs. After decades of refining the recipe, he felt he finally achieved his goal."

item.title: Mike Lull MV4 Modern Vintage Fretless- Fralin Pickups, Lusithand Preamp - Includes Hard Case — $2,529
item.description:
$2,529 · Excellent · Fret Factory

This Mike Lull MV4 Fretless is in Excellent condition. Some minor dings are present on the body, and there is one tiny crack in the finish near the bridge on the body front (all pictured) - none of these are noticeable or negatively affect the playability of the bass. Aftermarket Lusithand Preamp was pro-installed and in good working order, bridge hardware and tuning machines are in good shape. Includes hard shell case, original pickguard, and old electronics.

This Mike Lull Bass is a comfortable blend of modern & vintage - that's what the M and V stand for! This one is upgraded with a Lusithand Double NFP MKI active preamp (modeled after classic Alembic preamps) that features multiple EQ filters as well as an active/ passive tone switch! Aftermarket Fralin jazz pickups are the perfect match to this awesome preamp, this bass plays and sounds amazing! Not to mention it's VERY lightweight at just 7 lbs ~9oz!

item.title: 1974 Rickenbacker 4000 - Jetglo - With original Tole Case — $4,321.78
item.description:
$4,321.78 · Excellent · Andy's Vintage Guitars

Remarkable condition for this 52 years old Rickenbacker 4000 Bass in stunning jetglo finish.

These basses are very rare and they are considered the best sounding rickenbacker basses by many.

This one is no exception and sounds phenomenal .

Great weight, nice and comfortable neck profile, low action, great output, all original and in really great nick.

Comes with the original case and it,s ready to take the stage for the next 50 years.

One for players and collectors.

item.title: 1994 Ernie Ball Music Man Sterling Bass In Mint condition with original case — $2,796.44
item.description:
$2,796.44 · Excellent · Andy's Vintage Guitars

Another bass from my personal time machine. This Musicman Sterling Bass is an absolute stunner . Not to be confused with the Asian Musicman Sterling sub brand , this is the original USA Sterling model and it’s based on the stingray, but with a thinner neck and more tonal options. Perfect, not one mark or scuff equipped with the sought after original Flea high mass bridge with mutes. Gorgeous Birdseye maple neck and the sunburst finish is just spot on. Comes with the rare short tolex case which was only available for the first few months of production of this American Musicman Ernie Ball model. A great bass, as a player or collector piece. Renowned for its slightly smaller body and neck profile, the Sterling 4 string bass boasts a big sound. Nine volt active 3 band EQ preamp, 3 position pickup selector switch, and a ceramic humbucker with hum canceling phantom coil come standard, equipping the Sterling to cut through any mix. The optional addition of a pickup in the neck position adds even more versatility to this innovative bass. The Sterling delivers comfort, functionality, and the precision craftsmanship and attention to detail you’ve come to expect from Ernie Ball Music Man.

item.title: 1968 Rickenbacker 4001 in Fireglo Finish with original hard case — $14,490.66
item.description:
$14,490.66 · Very Good · Andy's Vintage Guitars

Absurdly rare, these 60s rickenbacker 4001 basses are some of the most sought after instruments on the planet , and for a good reason.

Completely unmolested and inside the original case, this bass is something else.

Crushed pearl inlays, checkerboard binding , toaster pickup, horseshoe pickup, mono jack input , bigger headstock , reversed kluson tuners.

All features only available through the 60s and discontinued the following decade when production ramped up.

Apparently only 83 fireglo 68 4001 were produced and this is a perfect example of the craftmanship and attention to details of those days.

Phenomenal tone, great weight and really nice neck profile.

The original case has seen better days and has been over sprayed but it’s still there and that’s all it matters.

item.title: 1968 Fender Precision Bass in rare Candy Apple Red Finish - With Original Hard case — $11,984.76
item.description:
$11,984.76 · Good · Andy's Vintage Guitars

Absolutely stunning and very rare custom candy Apple Red finish for this 58 years old Fender Precision bass. Light and resonant , with a sublime neck profile and the perfect Pbass tone, this bass is just a work of art. Nitro finish, Brazilian Rosewood fretboard and black bobbins pickups ( all features from the early preCBS days, but 68 was a transition year at Fender and they used all the leftovers, making some of the best instruments ever made). Let's not forget those gracious and rare Oval tuners aka Lollipop or Paddle, which were only available on the early 1968 Pbasses. Beside a new nut and a replaced screw on one of tuners, this instrument is completely original, with untouched solder joints, both covers still on and comes inside the original Stamford case. One for players and collectors, super rare and a superb player. Partial trade always welcome

item.title: FGN Neo Classic Bass NJB - 3 Color Sunburst — $716.37
item.description:
$716.37 · Mint · The Live Sound

Circle Fretting System (C.F.S.) — all strings cross the frets at 90°, ensuring even scale length and improving articulation and note definition.

Neck joint plate and stainless steel neck screws provide a fast response and tight, punchy sound.

Glow-in-the-dark side dots make it easy to see your position on dark stages.

Neck-end truss rod adjustment, which allows stable and easy access for setup without removing the neck


Speca

  • Construction: Bolt-on
  • Body: Alder
  • Neck: Maple (U-shape)
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood
  • Scale: 34” (864 mm)
  • Frets: 20 medium jumbo frets with C.F.S.
  • Tuners: Gotoh GB2
  • Bridge: Gotoh 203B-4 (string spacing 19 mm)
  • Hardware Finish: Chrome & Nickel
  • Neck Pickup: FGN 62J-F
  • Bridge Pickup: FGN 62J-R
  • Controls: 2 × Volume, 1 × Tone (with Series/Parallel push-pull switch)
  • Finish: Gloss
  • Strings: D’Addario EXL165 (.045–.105)
  • Accessories: Original Gig Bag
  • Colors: 3TS (3-Tone Sunburst), BK (Black)

item.title: Eastwood Univox Bass 2010s - Sunburst — $656.67
item.description:
$656.67 · Very Good · The Live Sound

Anybody who has played a good example of the original Univox will appreciate how resonant and light they were, and straight up rock n roll machines with more than a dash of punk thrown in… our reissue totally captures this, and, dare we say it, even goes beyond the original. We feel that our Univox Hi-Flier™ reissue is like finding the best possible example of an original, without the months/years of hit and miss searching!

We've been making the 6-string Univox Hi-Flier™ guitar for many years now, and figured they needed to have a 4-stringed big brother. So here it is! The Hi-Flier™ Bass.


  • Body: Basswood
  • Pickups: Two P-90's
  • Switching: 3-Way
  • Controls: 1 Volume 1 Tone
  • Bridge: Fender Style Adjustable, Rear String Mount, Chrome Cover
  • Neck: Maple, Bolt On
  • Finger Board: Maple, Dot Markers
  • Scale Length: 30 1/2"
  • Width at the Nut: 1 5/8"
  • Hardware: Gotoh style Style Nickel/Chrome
  • Strings: #45-#100 Medium Scale

item.title: 2023 Fender American Professional II Precision Bass Olympic White w/OHSC — $1,595
item.description:
$1,595 · Excellent · Imperial Vintage Guitars

2023 Fender American Professional II Precision Bass Olympic White w/OHSC

This Bass Is In Excellent Condition Showing Minimal Signs Of Use (See Photos)

Located At IVG Orange

Single V-Mod II Split-Coil Precision Bass Pickup

'63 P Bass neck profile

Bone Nut Width 1.64"

Neck Depth At 1st Fret 0.87", At 12th Fret 1.0"

20 Narrow-Tall Frets

HiMass Vintage Bridge 

Tapered-Shaft Tuning Machines

Posiflex Graphite Rods

Comes With Deluxe Molded Hardshell Case

The gold standard of modern Precision Basses, this 2023 Fender American Professional II Precision Bass in Olympic White combines legendary Fender tone with thoughtful modern refinements for today's working musician. Built in the USA, it features a comfortable '63 P Bass neck profile, rolled fingerboard edges, a smooth satin-finished neck, and a sculpted neck heel for effortless playability across the entire fretboard.

At its heart is Fender's acclaimed V-Mod II Precision Bass split-coil pickup, delivering the deep, punchy lows, rich midrange, and articulate definition that have made the Precision Bass the backbone of countless recordings and live performances. Whether you're playing rock, blues, country, funk, punk, or worship, this bass sits perfectly in the mix with the unmistakable sound only a P Bass can provide.

Finished in timeless Olympic White and complete with its Original Fender Hardshell Case, this American Professional II is equally at home on stage, in the studio, or as the centerpiece of a serious collection. With premium USA craftsmanship, exceptional comfort, and classic Fender styling, it's a professional-grade instrument built to perform for decades.

item.title: Fender Player Precision Bass with Pau Ferro Fretboard 2023 - 2024 - Candy Apple Red — $550
item.description:
$550 · Very Good · Chad's Gear Garage

Plays well and still has the plastic on it. $550 obo

item.title: Squier Classic Vibe '60s Jazz Bass 2025 - Present - Tahitian Coral — $389.99
item.description:
$389.99 · Very Good · American Jewelry and Loan

Pre-owned. Strummed on a bit before purchase and never used it. Still has the plastic on it. I didn't mark it as excellent because I noticed a chip next to the bridge pickup (photo 7). Otherwise in great shape. No hard case. Something appropriate will be provided if shipped.

From Fender:

A tribute to the decade of its birth, the Classic Vibe '60s Jazz Bass® combines the luxurious playability that made it famous with the versatility and massive tone of its dual Fender Designed™ alnico single-coil pickups. Player-friendly features include a slim, comfortable "C"-shaped neck profile with an easy-playing 9.5"-radius fingerboard and narrow-tall frets, as well as a vintage-style bridge with threaded saddles. This throwback Squier model also features 1960s-inspired headstock markings, rich-looking nickel-plated hardware and a slick vintage-tint gloss neck finish for an old-school vibe.

** No International Shipping **

** No Shipping To P.O. Boxes**

If you have questions, feel free to message us.

We are located at 1145 Baker St. Unit D Costa Mesa Ca. 92626.

We are open Tues.- Friday. 10:00am to 6:00pm,Sat. 12:00 to 5:00pm.

We offer local pickup.

item.title: 5-String J-Style Bass – Gilded Tundra Hand-Painted — $799
item.description:
$799 · Brand New · Dhatarattha Guitars USA

The DEB5 is a 5-string bass built for players who want modern playability, solid low-end response, and a unique visual style.

Finished in a Gilded Tundra Hand-Painted Finish with a high-gloss polyester finish, it features a mahogany body, roasted maple neck, rosewood fretboard, stainless steel frets, and a 3-band EQ system paired with hand-wound Laohao pickups.

The dual single-coil pickup configuration delivers tight lows, clear articulation, and enough top-end presence for slap, funk, rock, and modern jazz applications.


Every Gilded Tundra comes complete with a premium semi hard case for added protection during transport and storage.


The Tech Features

Body

Body Material: Mahogany

Body Finish: Gilded Tundra

Body Paint: Polyester

Body Construction: Solid

Neck

Neck Material: Roasted Maple

Neck Construction: One-Piece

Neck Joint: Bolt-On

Neck Finish: Satin

Neck Shape: C-shape

Scale Length: 864mm / 34"

Fretboard Material: Rosewood

Fretboard Radius: 350mm

Number of Frets: 20 Frets

Fret Material: Stainless Steel

Fret Size: Medium Jumbo

Nut Material: Bone

Nut Width: 45mm

Position Inlays: Block Inlays

Binding: N/A

Side Dots: Glow in the Dark

Truss Rod: Dual Action 2-Way

Electronics

Bridge Pickup: Laohao Hand Made Single-Coil Pickup

Middle Pickup: N/A

Neck Pickup: Laohao Hand Made Single-Coil Pickup

Switching: N/A

Configuration: SS

Special Electronics: 3-Band EQ

Hardware

Bridge: Hardtail

Hardware Finish: Chrome

Machine Heads: Chrome Open-Geared Clover-Style

Miscellaneous/Special Features

Strings: 45-130


Accessories

Included Accessories:  Truss Rod Adjustment Key, Bridge Adjustment Key, Strap Locks

item.title: Fender Player II Mustang Bass PJ - Rallye Orange — $849.99
item.description:
$849.99 · Brand New · Haggerty's Music

-YOU ARE VIEWING PHOTOS OF THE ACTUAL GUITAR BEING SOLD!

This brand new 7.4 lb Fender will ship insured, fully set up, and inspected from Rapid City, SD.

Buy risk free with our 14 day - full refund, less shipping, return policy. Check out our shop policies for more details.

The Fender Player II Mustang Bass PJ brings together classic Fender heritage and modern versatility in a compact package that’s built to perform. Comfortable, balanced, and incredibly fun to play, the Mustang Bass has long been a favorite for players who want a bass that feels effortless without sacrificing big, authoritative tone. Whether you’re holding down the groove in a rock band, laying down smooth lines in the studio, or exploring new musical styles, the Player II is ready for every gig.

The combination of Player Series Alnico V Precision Bass and Jazz Bass pickups gives this Mustang an impressively wide tonal range. From warm, punchy lows and growling mids to crisp articulation and clear definition, it adapts easily to just about any genre. The three-way pickup selector makes it simple to find your ideal voice, while the Modern “C” neck, rolled fingerboard edges, and satin finish create a smooth, broken-in feel that makes long rehearsals and performances feel effortless. Every detail is designed to inspire confidence, from the dependable bridge to the classic open-gear tuners that keep everything locked in tune.

Compact in size but anything but small in sound, the Player II Mustang Bass PJ is an instrument that encourages creativity every time you pick it up. It captures the unmistakable Fender character players have trusted for decades while adding thoughtful refinements for today’s musician. Available now at Haggerty’s Music.

Features / Specs

Highlights

  • Series: Player II
  • Orientation: Right-Handed
  • Color: Rallye Orange

Body

  • Body Material: Alder
  • Body Finish: Gloss Polyester
  • Body Shape: Mustang
  • Body Style: Solid

Neck

  • Headstock Type: Mustang
  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Neck Shape: Modern C
  • Neck Finish: Satin Urethane
  • Neck Depth: 0.868" at 1st Fret, 0.899" at 12th Fret
  • Neck Construction: Bolt-On
  • Fingerboard Radius: 9.5"
  • Fingerboard Material: Slab Rosewood
  • Position Inlays: White Dot
  • Side Dots: White
  • Number of Frets: 19
  • Truss Rod Style: Single Action, Head Adjust
  • Truss Rod Nut: Standard
  • Nut Material: Synthetic Bone
  • Nut Width: 1.5"
  • Fret Size: Medium Jumbo
  • Scale Length: 30"

Hardware

  • Bridge: 4-Saddle Standard
  • Bridge Cover/Tail Piece: None
  • Bridge Mounting: 5-Screw Vintage-Style
  • Pickguard: 3-Ply Parchment
  • Pickup Covers: Black
  • Control Knobs: Black Plastic with White Indicator
  • Switch Tip: Black
  • Hardware Finish: Nickel/Chrome
  • Tuning Machines: Standard Open-Gear
  • String Trees: Vintage-Style Disc
  • Strings: Fender USA 7250M Nickel Plated Steel (.045-.105 Gauges)
  • Tremolo Arm: None
  • Neck Plate: 4-Bolt
  • Strap Buttons: Two Standard
  • Output Jack: 1/4" Front

Electronics

  • Pickup Configuration: SS
  • Middle Pickup: Player Series Alnico 5 Split Single-Coil Precision Bass
  • Bridge Pickup: Player Series Alnico 5 Single-Coil Jazz Bass
  • Controls: Master Volume, Master Tone
  • Switching: 3-Way Toggle
    • Position 1. Bridge Pickup
    • Position 2. Bridge and Middle Pickups
    • Position 3. Middle Pickup
  • Auxiliary Switching: None

Miscellaneous

  • Accessories: Tools
  • Case/Gig Bag: Not Included
  • Serial Number: MX26048594
  • Manufacture Date: 2026

item.title: Brownsville Bass 1980s - Black — $70
item.description:
$70 · Good · Robert's Gear Emporium

Brownsville bass guitar. Full size. Numerous dings and scratches but still plays and sounds good .
